theme: bridge frame surviving sixteen different loadings
title: Sixteen-Load Truss Margin
seed_problem: single-load-case truss topology sizing under a material budget
mutation: composite: the objective is swept over 16 simultaneous load cases (gravity, four wind profiles, eleven moving point loads) and scored by the WORST-CASE-over-cases stress margin of a real linear-elastic truss solve, coupled to a member-selection + cross-section budget; a member only pays off if it survives every case, not the case a naive solver happens to check.
strategies: trivial: chords + verticals + one diagonal per bay (fixed direction), smallest cross-section everywhere -- the checker's own weak-but-stable reference
naive greedy: size cross-sections from GRAVITY alone (the single case carrying the most total load), upgrading the members gravity stresses hardest -- blind to the 15 other cases

insight (strong): test each candidate second diagonal's marginal effect on the WORST-CASE-over-16-cases margin directly, and rank cross-section upgrades by each member's worst utilization across the full case sweep -- finds the bays/members that sit at the intersection of many load paths instead of the ones gravity alone flags
a general solver can go further with iterative local search / LP relaxation over the full 16-case stress field
